  • GitHub

Architecture

Tether is built on Rust with a modular architecture. Each component is designed to be independently testable while working seamlessly together.

System Overview

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Your Laptop │ │ │ │ ┌──────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ ┌────────────┐ │ │ │ Axum HTTP │◄──►│ PTY Mgr │◄──►│ bash/sh │ │ │ │ + WS Srv │ │ (portable-pty)│ │ sessions │ │ │ └──────┬───────┘ └──────────────┘ └────────────┘ │ │ │ │ │ │ WebSocket (real-time I/O) │ │ ▼ │ │ ┌──────────────┐ │ │ │ Auth Mgr │ Argon2id + Session Cookies │ │ │ (argon2) │ Rate limiting: 5 attempts / 10 min │ │ └──────────────┘ │ └─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘ │ LAN (WiFi) ▼ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Mobile Web Browser (xterm.js) │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Core Components

1. HTTP/WS Server (server.rs)

Built on Axum, the server handles:

  • Static assets — Frontend files embedded via rust-embed
  • REST API — Terminal CRUD operations, auth endpoints
  • WebSocket upgrade — Real-time terminal I/O streaming
  • QR code generation — LAN access URL as scannable QR

2. PTY Manager (pty_manager.rs)

Uses portable-pty to manage pseudo-terminal sessions:

  • Spawn — Creates new PTY with shell process
  • Resize — Adjusts terminal dimensions on window resize
  • Write — Sends input to the shell
  • Read — Reads output from the shell
  • Kill — Gracefully terminates sessions

3. Auth Manager (auth.rs)

Implements secure authentication with:

  • Argon2id — Memory-hard password hashing (PHC winner)
  • Session cookies — UUID-based sessions with 7-day expiry
  • Rate limiting — 5 failed attempts per 10-minute window per IP

4. WebSocket Handler (ws_handler.rs)

Bridges WebSocket connections to PTY sessions:

  • Input direction — WS message → PTY writer
  • Output direction — PTY reader → WS message
  • Resize events — Frontend resize → PTY resize
  • Disconnect handling — Graceful cleanup on WS close

5. Application State (state.rs)

Shared state passed through Axum's dependency injection:

  • AppState — Holds Arc references to all managers
  • Clone derive — Required for Axum's extractor pattern

Data Flow

Terminal Input (Phone → Laptop)

User types on phone → xterm.js captures keystroke → Frontend sends WS text message → ws_handler receives message → PTY manager writes to pty writer → Shell process receives input

Terminal Output (Laptop → Phone)

Shell produces output → PTY reader receives bytes → ws_handler reads from PTY → Sends WS binary message → Frontend receives message → xterm.js renders output

Frontend Architecture

Key Technologies

TechnologyPurpose
xterm.jsTerminal emulation and rendering
FitAddonAuto-size terminal to container
WebGLAddonGPU-accelerated rendering
Vanilla JSNo framework overhead, fast load
CSS Grid/FlexResponsive mobile-first layout

UI Components

  • Terminal View — Full-screen xterm.js container
  • Preset Bar — Quick-tap command buttons
  • Console Drawer — Swipeable panel for terminal management
  • Custom Input — Text field for arbitrary commands
  • Notification Toast — Input-wait alerts

Project Structure

tether/ ├── Cargo.toml # Rust dependencies ├── src/ │ ├── main.rs # CLI entry point (clap) │ ├── server.rs # Axum HTTP server + routing │ ├── pty_manager.rs # PTY lifecycle management │ ├── auth.rs # Argon2 auth + session management │ ├── ws_handler.rs # WebSocket ↔ PTY bridge │ └── state.rs # Shared application state ├── assets/ │ ├── index.html # Main dashboard │ ├── login.html # Password gate │ ├── css/style.css # Mobile-first styles │ └── js/app.js # Frontend logic └── .github/ ├── workflows/ # CI/CD pipelines └── pages/ # GitHub Pages docs site
📖 Want to dive deeper? Check out the source code on GitHub to explore the implementation details.
